responses.rawOutput: {"final_answer":["1. No, the sprinklers do not run this cycle (tank is at 15%, which is ≤20%).","2. Yes, the refill valve activates to fill the tank from mains supply.","3. The pump draws from the tank, but since the tank is at 15% (≤20%), the pump activates the refill valve instead of sending water to sprinklers. So the refill valve activates and no water goes to the sprinklers."],"confidence":0.97,"working":["Q1: Rain sensor reports no rain, so timer activates pump at 6 AM. Tank is 15%, which is ≤20%, so pump does NOT send water to sprinklers.","Q2: Since tank ≤20%, pump activates refill valve, which fills tank from mains. So yes, refill valve activates.","Q3: Without rain sensor, timer always fires at 6 AM. Pump activates. Tank at 15% ≤20%, so pump activates refill valve, no sprinklers. Same outcome as Q1/Q2: refill valve on, sprinklers off."]}

final_answer: 1. No, the sprinklers do not run this cycle (tank is at 15%, which is ≤20%).
2. Yes, the refill valve activates to fill the tank from mains supply.

3. With the rain sensor removed, the timer activates the pump at 6 AM regardless of rain. The pump draws from the tank, but since the tank is at 15% (≤20%), the pump activates the refill valve instead of sending water to sprinklers. So the refill valve activates and no water goes to the sprinklers.
working: Q1: Rain sensor reports no rain, so timer activates pump at 6 AM. Tank is 15%, which is ≤20%, so pump does NOT send water to sprinklers.
Q2: Since tank ≤20%, pump activates refill valve, which fills tank from mains. So yes, refill valve activates.
Q3: Without rain sensor, timer always fires at 6 AM. Pump activates. Tank at 15% ≤20%, so pump activates refill valve, no sprinklers. Same outcome as Q1/Q2: refill valve on, sprinklers off.
